title: Golf day to support the disadvantaged youth
description: GEORGE NEWS - Local Motion is thrilled to...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - Local Motion is thrilled to invite everyone to an exciting golf day on Friday 6 September at the beautiful Kingswood Golf Club. This event promises a fantastic day of golfing while raising essential funds for Local Motion's various projects.


> Local Motion is an organisation dedicated to creating opportunities for young people, helping them find purpose, become leaders, and make a positive impact in their communities.


They support youth through a variety of programmes such as extra homework and math classes, surf lessons, art and culture activities, devotion and discipleship programmes, student camps, and career guidance sessions with guest speakers.

Participating in the golf day is a great way to support these initiatives.

You can join by entering a team of four, sponsoring a team, setting up a gazebo for your business at one of the holes, or donating prizes.

They prefer prizes in sets of four to match the 4-ball format of the event.

To learn more about Local Motion and their work, you can follow them on Instagram (@localmotionza) and Facebook, or visit their website at www.localmotionza.org.

For more details about the golf day feel free to contact them on 083 253 9744.

Tesneen Lewis (left), Devon Prins and Sabrina Beswick (team leader) during a parkrun at the botanical garden.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
